name: put payload
check data
## Instructions ##
info:
===== Simulated Zendure Devices =====
== version 20260413 ==

In this Node-RED flow there are several simulated Zendure devices available for testing.

With this you can for example already try out a Zendure Home Battery with the Gielz Home Assistant Integration even if you do not own one.

This Node-RED flow also has a simulated HomeWizard P1 (home power) meter available, which can also be used by the Gielz Home Assistant Integration. 

You can use the live data from your real P1 meter or from an MQTT data feed, to base the simulated P1 meter on. The simulated meter will 
add/subtract the power from the simulated Zendure batteries from that value, so that you can do a realistic simulation of Smart Matching.



==== How to use these simulated Zendure devices:

1) From the Gielz Home Assistant dashboard:
Connect with the simulated Zendure device on:
<node-red_ip_adres>:1880/testdevice1
or 
<node-red_ip_adres>:1880/endpoint/testdevice1

The simulated HomeWizard P1 can be entered in the Gielz dashboard as:
<node-red_ip_adres>:1880/endpoint
of
<node-red_ip_adres>:1880

Note: It depends on your installation whether or not you should include "/endpoint" in the address line.
You can check this by checking http-in nodes such as "testdevice1 - SF2400AC - GET requests in". 
If in such node it says "The url will be relative to /endpoint.", then you need to include /endpoint.

2) From the Proxy:
In the node "Vul hier de Zendure device IP adressen in", in stead of device IP adreses, enter the name
of the simulated devices which you wish to use.

//===============================================================
//== Vul hier de Zendure device IP adressen in ==
//===============================================================

let ipZendure1 = "testdevice1"
let ipZendure2 = "testdevice2"
let ipZendure3 = "testdevice4"

==== Available simulated devices:
testdevice1 - Solarflow 2400AC
testdevice2 - Solarflow 2400AC
testdevice3 - Solarflow 2400AC
testdevice4 - Solarflow 2400AC+


3) Direct access to the ZenSDK API (e.g. curl or browser)
http://<node-red_ip_adres>:1880/endpoint/testdevice1/properties/report
or
http://<node-red_ip_adres>:1880/testdevice1/properties/report

put payload

Set real HomeWizard IP address

set the SoC

Simulated Homewizard P1 meter (can be used for testing, smart matching (NOM), e.g. if you do not have a home battery in use already).
Optionally the meter can be based on the live data from your real Homewizard P1 meter or an MQTT feed. The simulated devices power will be added/subtracted.
Set P1 meter to zero Watts
set the Power to zero
save MQTT power value
Enable the MQTT node and enter the topic if you wish to get the live data from an MQTT feed
